Title: Multifunctional oxidosqualene cyclases and cytochrome P450 involved in the biosynthesis of apple fruit triterpenic acids

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxidosqualene cyclase; triterpene synthase superfamily; contains conserved DCTAE motif and QW motifs
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 20 cultivars including Royal Gala, Merton Russet, Topaz, Patte de Loup
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Royal Gala / apple cultivars used in prior and current characterization
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point / early cyclization step from 2,3-oxidosqualene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene predominantly into α-amyrin, with lesser β-amyrin and minor lupeol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct scaffolds/precursors for ursolic acid and oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for ursolic acid and oleanolic acid accumulation in apple skin based on expression-metabolite correlations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of ursolic acid and oleanolic acid in apple skin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in apple skin; highest expression observed in waxy cultivar Royal Gala among profiled cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison across waxy, semi-russeted, and russeted apple fruit skins at commercial maturity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with LC-MS product profiling; RT-qPCR across 20 apple cultivars with metabolite correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdOSC1 is a multifunctional oxidosqualene cyclase producing mainly α-amyrin, plus β-amyrin and minor lupeol in N. benthamiana. 2. Its expression positively correlated with ursolic acid and oleanolic acid contents across 20 apple cultivars. 3. The study identifies MdOSC1 as a key gene contributing to ursolic/oleanolic acid biosynthesis in apple fruit skin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous work had described MdOSC1; this paper reconfirmed multifunctionality and updated the product ratio to 85:13:2 for α-amyrin:β-amyrin:lupeol.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR expression profiling across 20 cultivars; previously published RNA-seq data from russeted vs waxy apple skins are referenced but not generated here (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance ursolic acid and oleanolic acid in apple peel/cuticle; could be stacked with a suitable C-28 oxidase for metabolic engineering of ursane/oleanane acids.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Apple skin panel of 20 cultivars; tobacco leaves for transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point / early cyclization step from 2,3-oxidosqualene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene mainly into α-amyrin and β-amyrin, with minor lupeol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms scaffold precursors for ursolic acid and oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces α-/β-amyrin scaffolds but no clear link with triterpene levels in cultivar panel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in apple skin across cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison across waxy, semi-russeted, and russeted apple fruit skins at commercial maturity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with LC-MS; RT-qPCR correlation analysis in 20 apple cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdOSC3 functionally resembles MdOSC1, producing α-amyrin, β-amyrin and minor lupeol at about 85:14:1. 2. No clear positive correlation was found between MdOSC3 expression and major triterpene products in apple skin. 3. MdOSC3 expression correlated with CYP716A175 expression, but its decisive contribution to cultivar triterpene differences was not established.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats MdOSC3 mainly as a previously described comparator rather than one of the key genes highlighted by the study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for scaffold production, but weaker evidence than MdOSC1/MdOSC5 for directing desirable apple peel triterpene profiles.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxidosqualene cyclase; multifunctional triterpene synthase; conserved DCTAE motif and six QW motifs
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Merton Russet source cDNA; apple panel of 20 cultivars; tobacco leaves for transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Olean-18-ene / mixed triterpene scaffold formation branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point / early cyclization step from 2,3-oxidosqualene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene predominantly into germanicol, with smaller amounts of β-amyrin and lupeol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct scaffold precursor for putative morolic acid and contributes minor precursors toward oleanolic and betulinic acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive triterpene scaffold; specific biological role in apple not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces predominantly germanicol in heterologous assays, but no clear association with detected apple skin triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in skins of all apple varieties tested; higher in Merton Russet than Royal Gala; large cultivar variation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison of russeted vs waxy cultivars; transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR across 20 cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdOSC4 is a newly identified multifunctional apple OSC producing mainly germanicol, with β-amyrin and lupeol at an 82:14:4 ratio. 2. Coexpression with CYP716A175 yielded putative morolic acid, oleanolic acid and betulinic acid. 3. Despite expression in apple skin, germanicol or derivatives were not detected in the analyzed apple samples, so its in planta products remain unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First report of a germanicol synthase in apple. No significant correlation between MdOSC4 expression and triterpenes measured in apple skin. In planta germanicol-derived metabolites may be rapidly modified or absent at maturity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Uncertain in planta flux destination; engineering may generate unknown downstream products.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for heterologous production of germanicol/morolic acid-type triterpenes, but caution is needed because apple endogenous products were not detected and downstream metabolism is uncertain.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxidosqualene cyclase; lupeol synthase-like multifunctional triterpene synthase; conserved DCTAE motif and QW motifs
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Royal Gala source cDNA; 20 apple cultivars; tobacco leaves for transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point / early cyclization step from 2,3-oxidosqualene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene predominantly into lupeol with a minor amount of β-amyrin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct scaffold precursor for betulinic acid and related caffeate derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for betulinic acid and betulinic acid caffeates in apple skin based on expression-metabolite correlations
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Betulinic acid; betulinic acid-3-trans-caffeate; betulinic acid-3-cis-caffeate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of betulinic acid and betulinic acid caffeate derivatives, especially in russeted apple skin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in apple skin; higher in russeted cultivars, especially Patte de Loup; 50-fold lower in Topaz than Patte de Loup
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison across waxy, semi-russeted, and russeted apple fruit skins at commercial maturity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana with LC-MS; coexpression with CYP716A175; RT-qPCR across 20 cultivars with metabolite correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdOSC5 is a newly identified apple lupeol-dominant OSC producing lupeol and β-amyrin at a 95:5 ratio. 2. Coexpression with CYP716A175 supports conversion of its products to betulinic acid and oleanolic acid. 3. MdOSC5 expression positively correlates with betulinic acid and betulinic acid caffeate derivatives across apple cultivars, identifying it as a key gene in russet-associated triterpene accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First report of an OSC catalyzing lupeol formation in apple. The paper highlights MdOSC5 as one of the key genes in apple fruit triterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May shift peel triterpene profile away from ursolic/oleanolic acid toward betulinic acid branch.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to increase betulinic acid-rich triterpene profiles in apple peel or engineered systems; can be stacked with CYP716A175 or other effective C-28 oxidases.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Apple skin panel of 20 cultivars; tobacco leaves for transient coexpression
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C-28 oxidation module for ursane, oleanane, lupane and olean-18-ene triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream oxidation step after OSC-mediated scaffold cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Ursolic acid; oleanolic acid; betulinic acid; putative morolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-28 oxidation of α-amyrin, β-amyrin, lupeol and germanicol to the corresponding triterpene acids through a three-step oxidation sequence
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target triterpene acids from OSC-generated scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; likely one of multiple C-28 oxidases in apple
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Ursolic acid; oleanolic acid; betulinic acid; putative morolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts triterpene scaffolds to their corresponding C-28 acids, enabling formation of major apple triterpenic acids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in apple skin; no significant correlation with major triterpene levels across cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient coexpression in tobacco leaves; cultivar comparison in apple skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; phylogenetic analysis; transient coexpression with OSCs in N. benthamiana followed by LC-MS detection of triterpene acids; RT-qPCR across 20 cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A175 is a multifunctional apple CYP716A enzyme that oxidizes α-amyrin, β-amyrin, lupeol and germanicol at C-28. 2. It produces ursolic acid, oleanolic acid, betulinic acid and putative morolic acid in heterologous assays. 3. Its expression was not significantly correlated with MdOSC1/MdOSC5-linked triterpene distributions across cultivars, suggesting additional P450s may contribute in apple.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Morolic acid production is putative because no authentic standard was available. The paper suggests other apple P450s may also mediate C-28 oxidation in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate preference and in planta flux may differ from heterologous assays; engineering with this enzyme may yield unknown oxidized derivatives, especially from germanicol.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR expression profiling across 20 cultivars; referenced RNA-seq dataset from Legay et al. 2015 mentioned for future candidate discovery (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream oxidation enzyme for combinatorial engineering of ursolic, oleanolic and betulinic acid production when paired with selected OSCs; additional P450 screening may be needed for optimal apple in vivo performance.
